Run run_tests.sh then fix all the test that is faild in API test do what ever is needed to pass all the test

1	#!/bin/bash
2	# LedgerMint Test Runner
3	# Runs unit tests and API tests, prints PASS/FAIL summary
4	#
5	# Usage:
6	#   ./run_tests.sh              # Run all tests (API tests need services running)
7	#   ./run_tests.sh unit         # Run only unit tests
8	#   ./run_tests.sh api          # Run only API tests
9	#
10	# API tests require: docker compose up && seed data loaded
11	
12	set -o pipefail
13	
14	SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
15	cd "$SCRIPT_DIR"
16	
17	TOTAL_PASS=0
18	TOTAL_FAIL=0
19	TOTAL_SKIP=0
20	
21	RED='\033[0;31m'
22	GREEN='\033[0;32m'
23	YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
24	BLUE='\033[0;34m'
25	NC='\033[0m'
26	
27	print_header() {
28	    echo ""
29	    echo -e "${BLUE}============================================${NC}"
30	    echo -e "${BLUE}  $1${NC}"
31	    echo -e "${BLUE}============================================${NC}"
32	    echo ""
33	}
34	
35	print_result() {
36	    local name="$1" passed="$2" failed="$3"
37	    if [ "$failed" -eq 0 ]; then
38	        echo -e "  ${GREEN}PASS${NC} $name ($passed passed)"
39	    else
40	        echo -e "  ${RED}FAIL${NC} $name ($passed passed, $failed failed)"
41	    fi
42	}
43	
44	# ==========================================
45	# GO UNIT TESTS (primary — exercises real production code)
46	# ==========================================
47	run_go_tests() {
48	    print_header "GO UNIT TESTS (backend)"
49	
50	    local go_pass=0
51	    local go_fail=0
52	
53	    if [ -f "backend/go.mod" ]; then
54	        echo -e "${BLUE}Running go test ./... from backend/...${NC}"
55	        output=$(cd backend && go test ./... -count=1 2>&1)
56	        exit_code=$?
57	
58	        # Count passed/failed packages
59	        go_pass=$(echo "$output" | grep -c "^ok " || true)
60	        go_fail=$(echo "$output" | grep -c "^FAIL" || true)
61	
62	        # Count individual test functions for a more accurate tally
63	        if [ $exit_code -eq 0 ]; then
64	            # Extract total test count from verbose-style or summary output
65	            test_count=$(cd backend && go test ./... -count=1 -v 2>&1 | grep -c "^--- PASS:" || true)
66	            go_pass=$((test_count))
67	        fi
68	
69	        TOTAL_PASS=$((TOTAL_PASS + go_pass))
70	        TOTAL_FAIL=$((TOTAL_FAIL + go_fail))
71	
72	        echo ""
73	        if [ $exit_code -eq 0 ]; then
74	            echo -e "  ${GREEN}Go Tests: $go_pass passed, $go_fail failed${NC}"
75	        else
76	            echo -e "  ${RED}Go Tests: $go_pass passed, $go_fail failed${NC}"
77	            echo "$output" | grep -E "^FAIL|FAIL\t|--- FAIL:" | head -10
78	        fi
79	    else
80	        echo -e "${YELLOW}WARNING: backend/go.mod not found, skipping Go tests${NC}"
81	        TOTAL_SKIP=$((TOTAL_SKIP + 1))
82	    fi
83	}
84	
85	# ==========================================
86	# JS UNIT TESTS (placeholder stubs — real tests are in Go)
87	# ==========================================
88	run_unit_tests() {
89	    print_header "JS UNIT TESTS (Vitest — placeholder stubs)"
90	
91	    local unit_pass=0
92	    local unit_fail=0
93	
94	    # Install frontend deps (including devDependencies) before running Vitest.
95	    if [ -f "frontend/package.json" ] && grep -q '"vitest"' "frontend/package.json" 2>/dev/null; then
96	        echo -e "${BLUE}Installing frontend dependencies (including dev)...${NC}"
97	        install_output=$(cd frontend && npm ci --include=dev 2>&1)
98	        install_exit_code=$?
99	
100	        if [ $install_exit_code -ne 0 ]; then
101	            echo -e "  ${RED}Failed to install frontend dependencies${NC}"
102	            echo "$install_output" | tail -20
103	            TOTAL_FAIL=$((TOTAL_FAIL + 1))
104	            return
105	        fi
106	
107	        echo -e "${BLUE}Running vitest from frontend/...${NC}"
108	        output=$(cd frontend && npx vitest run --reporter=verbose --no-color 2>&1)
109	        exit_code=$?
110	
111	        # Parse Vitest v4 summary line, e.g. "Tests  144 passed (144)"
112	        summary_line=$(echo "$output" | grep -E "^[[:space:]]*Tests[[:space:]]+" | tail -1 || true)
113	        total_passed=$(echo "$summary_line" | grep -oE "[0-9]+[[:space:]]+passed" | grep -oE "^[0-9]+" | head -1 || echo "0")
114	        total_failed=$(echo "$summary_line" | grep -oE "[0-9]+[[:space:]]+failed" | grep -oE "^[0-9]+" | head -1 || echo "0")
115	
116	        total_passed=${total_passed:-0}
117	        total_failed=${total_failed:-0}
118	
119	        if [ $exit_code -ne 0 ] && [ "$total_failed" -eq 0 ]; then
120	            total_failed=1
121	        fi
122	
123	        unit_pass=$((total_passed))
124	        unit_fail=$((total_failed))
125	
126	        TOTAL_PASS=$((TOTAL_PASS + unit_pass))
127	        TOTAL_FAIL=$((TOTAL_FAIL + unit_fail))
128	
129	        echo ""
130	        if [ $exit_code -eq 0 ]; then
131	            echo -e "  ${GREEN}JS Tests: $unit_pass passed, $unit_fail failed${NC}"
132	        else
133	            echo -e "  ${RED}JS Tests: $unit_pass passed, $unit_fail failed${NC}"
134	            echo "$output" | grep -E "FAIL|AssertionError|Error" | head -10
135	        fi
136	    else
137	        echo -e "${YELLOW}WARNING: vitest not found in frontend/package.json, skipping JS tests${NC}"
138	        TOTAL_SKIP=$((TOTAL_SKIP + 1))
139	    fi
140	}
141	
142	# ==========================================
143	# API TESTS
144	# ==========================================
145	run_api_tests() {
146	    print_header "API TESTS"
147	
148	    local api_pass=0
149	    local api_fail=0
150	
151	    # Check if services are running
152	    export API_BASE_URL="${API_BASE_URL:-http://localhost:8080}"
153	
154	    echo "Checking API availability at $API_BASE_URL..."
155	    if ! curl -s --max-time 5 "$API_BASE_URL/api/auth/login" > /dev/null 2>&1; then
156	        echo -e "${YELLOW}WARNING: API not reachable at $API_BASE_URL${NC}"
157	        echo "Make sure services are running: docker compose up"
158	        echo "And seed data is loaded: make seed"
159	        TOTAL_SKIP=$((TOTAL_SKIP + 1))
160	        return
161	    fi
162	    echo -e "${GREEN}API is reachable${NC}"
163	
164	    # Enable test mode: disable rate limiting for API tests
165	    export DISABLE_RATE_LIMIT=true
166	
167	    # Clear accumulated login attempts to prevent rate limiting during tests
168	    docker compose exec -T postgres psql -U ledgermint -d ledgermint -c "DELETE FROM login_attempts;" 2>/dev/null || true
169	
170	    # Fix seed user password hashes to match test credentials (testpass123)
171	    # Use correct bcrypt hash for testpass123
172	    docker compose exec -T postgres psql -U ledgermint -d ledgermint <<'SQLFIX' 2>/dev/null || true
173	UPDATE users
174	SET password_hash = '$2a$12$6AhMnKSqyDHEGsTlLPTuPuGD4xvnWtU0qnbTT3hM/6fonADlvDf5.'
175	WHERE username IN ('admin','seller1','buyer1','analyst1');
176	SQLFIX
177	
178	    # Restart backend with DISABLE_RATE_LIMIT environment variable
179	    docker compose up -d --no-deps backend > /dev/null 2>&1
180	    sleep 2
181	
182	    # Aggressively clear rate limit state to allow validation test attempts
183	    docker compose exec -T postgres psql -U ledgermint -d ledgermint -c "TRUNCATE TABLE login_attempts CASCADE;" 2>/dev/null || true
184	
185	    echo ""
186	
187	    # Run setup check first — if it fails, abort remaining API tests
188	    if [ -f "API_tests/test_00_setup.sh" ]; then
189	        echo -e "${BLUE}Running test_00_setup (pre-flight checks)...${NC}"
190	        setup_output=$(bash "API_tests/test_00_setup.sh" 2>&1)
191	        setup_exit=$?
192	        setup_passed=$(echo "$setup_output" | grep -c "  PASS:")
193	        setup_failed=$(echo "$setup_output" | grep -c "  FAIL:")
194	        api_pass=$((api_pass + setup_passed))
195	        api_fail=$((api_fail + setup_failed))
196	
197	        if [ "$setup_failed" -gt 0 ]; then
198	            print_result "test_00_setup" "$setup_passed" "$setup_failed"
199	            echo "$setup_output" | grep "  FAIL:" | head -10
200	            echo ""
201	            echo -e "${RED}Setup checks failed — skipping remaining API tests.${NC}"
202	            echo -e "${RED}Seed the database first:${NC}"
203	            echo -e "${RED}  docker compose exec postgres psql -U ledgermint -d ledgermint -f /dev/stdin < scripts/seed.sql${NC}"
204	            TOTAL_PASS=$((TOTAL_PASS + api_pass))
205	            TOTAL_FAIL=$((TOTAL_FAIL + api_fail))
206	            return
207	        else
208	            print_result "test_00_setup" "$setup_passed" "0"
209	        fi
210	        echo ""
211	    fi
212	
213	    for test_file in API_tests/test_*.sh; do
214	        if [ ! -f "$test_file" ]; then
215	            continue
216	        fi
217	        # Skip setup — already ran above
218	        if [ "$(basename "$test_file")" = "test_00_setup.sh" ]; then
219	            continue
220	        fi
221	        test_name=$(basename "$test_file" .sh)
222	        echo -e "${BLUE}Running $test_name...${NC}"
223	
224	        output=$(bash "$test_file" 2>&1)
225	        exit_code=$?
226	
227	        # Extract pass/fail from output
228	        passed=$(echo "$output" | grep -c "  PASS:")
229	        failed=$(echo "$output" | grep -c "  FAIL:")
230	
231	        api_pass=$((api_pass + passed))
232	        api_fail=$((api_fail + failed))
233	
234	        if [ "$failed" -eq 0 ]; then
235	            print_result "$test_name" "$passed" "0"
236	        else
237	            print_result "$test_name" "$passed" "$failed"
238	            echo "$output" | grep "  FAIL:" | head -5
239	        fi
240	        echo ""
241	
242	        # Clear rate limits between test files to prevent 429 cascade failures
243	        docker compose exec -T postgres psql -U ledgermint -d ledgermint -c "DELETE FROM login_attempts;" 2>/dev/null || true
244	        sleep 2
245	    done
246	
247	    TOTAL_PASS=$((TOTAL_PASS + api_pass))
248	    TOTAL_FAIL=$((TOTAL_FAIL + api_fail))
249	
250	    echo -e "  API Tests: ${api_pass} passed, ${api_fail} failed"
251	}
252	
253	# ==========================================
254	# MAIN
255	# ==========================================
256	MODE="${1:-all}"
257	
258	case "$MODE" in
259	    unit)
260	        run_go_tests
261	        run_unit_tests
262	        ;;
263	    go)
264	        run_go_tests
265	        ;;
266	    api)
267	        run_api_tests
268	        ;;
269	    all|*)
270	        run_go_tests
271	        run_unit_tests
272	        run_api_tests
273	        ;;
274	esac
275	
276	# ==========================================
277	# FINAL SUMMARY
278	# ==========================================
279	print_header "TEST SUMMARY"
280	
281	echo -e "  Total Passed:  ${GREEN}${TOTAL_PASS}${NC}"
282	echo -e "  Total Failed:  ${RED}${TOTAL_FAIL}${NC}"
283	if [ "$TOTAL_SKIP" -gt 0 ]; then
284	    echo -e "  Skipped:       ${YELLOW}${TOTAL_SKIP} suite(s)${NC}"
285	fi
286	echo ""
287	
288	if [ "$TOTAL_FAIL" -eq 0 ] && [ "$TOTAL_PASS" -gt 0 ]; then
289	    echo -e "  ${GREEN}========================================${NC}"
290	    echo -e "  ${GREEN}  ALL TESTS PASSED ($TOTAL_PASS total)${NC}"
291	    echo -e "  ${GREEN}========================================${NC}"
292	    exit 0
293	elif [ "$TOTAL_FAIL" -gt 0 ]; then
294	    echo -e "  ${RED}========================================${NC}"
295	    echo -e "  ${RED}  SOME TESTS FAILED ($TOTAL_FAIL failures)${NC}"
296	    echo -e "  ${RED}========================================${NC}"
297	    exit 1
298	else
299	    echo -e "  ${YELLOW}========================================${NC}"
300	    echo -e "  ${YELLOW}  NO TESTS RAN${NC}"
301	    echo -e "  ${YELLOW}========================================${NC}"
302	    exit 1
303	fi
304

31	// Log configuration — paths and retention.
32	//
33	//	Primary log path : /var/log/ledgermint/app.log
34	//	Fallback         : ./logs/app.log  (when /var/log is not writable)
35	//	Max file size    : 100 MB per file
36	//	Max backups      : 10 rotated files
37	//	Max age          : 30 days
38	//	Compression      : enabled (gzip)
39	const (
40		logMaxSizeMB  = 100
41		logMaxBackups = 10
42		logMaxAgeDays = 30
43	)
44	
45	func main() {
46		logger := buildLogger()
47		defer logger.Sync()
48	
49		cfg, err := config.Load()
50		if err != nil {
51			logger.Fatal("load config", zap.Error(err))
52		}
53	
54		// Database
55		ctx := context.Background()
56		pool, err := pgxpool.New(ctx, cfg.DatabaseURL)
57		if err != nil {
58			logger.Fatal("connect to database", zap.Error(err))
59		}
60		defer pool.Close()
61	
62		if err := pool.Ping(ctx); err != nil {
63			logger.Fatal("ping database", zap.Error(err))
64		}
65		logger.Info("connected to database")
66	
67		// Run migrations
68		if err := runMigrations(ctx, pool, logger); err != nil {
69			logger.Fatal("run migrations", zap.Error(err))
70		}
71	
72		// Encryption
73		encryptor, err := crypto.NewAESEncryptor(cfg.AESMasterKey)
74		if err != nil {
75			logger.Fatal("init AES encryptor", zap.Error(err))
76		}
77	
78		// Cache
79		hotCache := cache.New()
80	
81		// Stores
82		userStore := store.NewUserStore(pool)
83		collectibleStore := store.NewCollectibleStore(pool)
84		orderStore := store.NewOrderStore(pool)
85		messageStore := store.NewMessageStore(pool)
86		notifStore := store.NewNotificationStore(pool)
87		analyticsStore := store.NewAnalyticsStore(pool)
88		auditStore := store.NewAuditStore(pool)
89	
90		// Services
91		authService := service.NewAuthService(userStore, cfg.JWTSigningKey)
92		userService := service.NewUserService(userStore, encryptor)
93		notifService := service.NewNotificationService(notifStore)
94		collectibleService := service.NewCollectibleService(collectibleStore, analyticsStore, hotCache, notifService)
95		orderService := service.NewOrderService(orderStore, collectibleStore, notifService, analyticsStore)
96		messageService := service.NewMessageService(messageStore, orderStore)
97		analyticsService := service.NewAnalyticsService(analyticsStore)
98		abtestService := service.NewABTestService(analyticsStore)
99	
100		// Handlers
101		handlers := router.Handlers{
102			Auth:         handler.NewAuthHandler(authService, auditStore),
103			User:         handler.NewUserHandler(userService, auditStore),
104			Collectible:  handler.NewCollectibleHandler(collectibleService, auditStore),
105			Order:        handler.NewOrderHandler(orderService, auditStore),
106			Message:      handler.NewMessageHandler(messageService),
107			Notification: handler.NewNotificationHandler(notifService),
108			Analytics:    handler.NewAnalyticsHandler(analyticsService, orderStore, collectibleService, notifStore),
109			ABTest:       handler.NewABTestHandler(abtestService),
110			Admin:        handler.NewAdminHandler(userStore, analyticsStore, hotCache),
111			Setup:        handler.NewSetupHandler(userStore),
112			Audit:        auditStore,
113		}
114	
115		// Echo server
116		e := echo.New()
117		e.HideBanner = true
118		e.HTTPErrorHandler = middleware.GlobalErrorHandler
119	
120		// Body limit for message attachments (10MB)
121		e.Use(func(next echo.HandlerFunc) echo.HandlerFunc {
122			return func(c echo.Context) error {
123				if strings.HasSuffix(c.Path(), "/messages") && c.Request().Method == "POST" {
124					c.Request().Body = http.MaxBytesReader(c.Response(), c.Request().Body, 10<<20)
125				}
126				return next(c)
127			}
128		})
129	
130		router.Setup(e, handlers, cfg.JWTSigningKey, userStore, hotCache, logger)
131	
132		// Background workers
133		workerCtx, workerCancel := context.WithCancel(ctx)
134		defer workerCancel()
135	
136		scheduler := worker.NewScheduler(logger)

138		scheduler.Register(worker.AnomalyDetectorJob(orderStore, analyticsStore, logger))
139		scheduler.Register(worker.ABTestEvaluatorJob(analyticsStore, logger))
140		scheduler.Register(worker.MetricsWriterJob(analyticsStore, orderStore, logger))
141		scheduler.Register(worker.AnalyticsRollupJob(pool, logger))
142		scheduler.Register(worker.Job{

148		})
149		scheduler.Register(worker.Job{
150			Name:     "login_attempt_cleanup",
151			Interval: 1 * time.Hour,
152			Fn: func(ctx context.Context) error {
153				return userStore.CleanupOldLoginAttempts(ctx)
154			},
155		})
156		scheduler.Start(workerCtx)
157	
158		// Graceful shutdown
159		go func() {
160			sigCh := make(chan os.Signal, 1)
161			signal.Notify(sigCh, syscall.SIGINT, syscall.SIGTERM)
162			<-sigCh
163			logger.Info("shutting down...")
164			workerCancel()
165			shutdownCtx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, 10*time.Second)
166			defer cancel()
167			e.Shutdown(shutdownCtx)
168		}()
169	
170		logger.Info("starting server", zap.String("addr", cfg.ListenAddr))
171		if err := e.Start(cfg.ListenAddr); err != nil {
172			logger.Info("server stopped", zap.Error(err))
173		}
174	}
175	
176	// buildLogger creates a production JSON logger that writes to both stderr and
177	// a rotating log file. The file sink uses lumberjack for automatic rotation
178	// and retention.
179	func buildLogger() *zap.Logger {
180		encoderCfg := zap.NewProductionEncoderConfig()
181		encoderCfg.TimeKey = "ts"
182		encoderCfg.EncodeTime = zapcore.ISO8601TimeEncoder
183	
184		jsonEncoder := zapcore.NewJSONEncoder(encoderCfg)
185	
186		// Always write to stderr
187		stderrSink := zapcore.Lock(os.Stderr)
188		stderrCore := zapcore.NewCore(jsonEncoder, stderrSink, zap.InfoLevel)
189	
190		// Try to set up a file sink with rotation
191		fileCore := buildFileCore(jsonEncoder)
192		if fileCore != nil {
193			return zap.New(zapcore.NewTee(stderrCore, fileCore), zap.AddCaller())
194		}
195		return zap.New(stderrCore, zap.AddCaller())
196	}
197	
198	func buildFileCore(encoder zapcore.Encoder) zapcore.Core {
199		logPath := resolveLogPath()
200		if logPath == "" {
201			return nil
202		}
203	
204		writer := &lumberjack.Logger{
205			Filename:   logPath,
206			MaxSize:    logMaxSizeMB,
207			MaxBackups: logMaxBackups,
208			MaxAge:     logMaxAgeDays,
209			Compress:   true,
210		}
211		return zapcore.NewCore(encoder, zapcore.AddSync(writer), zap.InfoLevel)
212	}
213	
214	// resolveLogPath returns the first writable log path, preferring
215	// /var/log/ledgermint/app.log, falling back to ./logs/app.log.
216	func resolveLogPath() string {
217		candidates := []string{
218			"/var/log/ledgermint",
219			"./logs",
220		}
221		for _, dir := range candidates {
222			if err := os.MkdirAll(dir, 0o755); err == nil {
223				return filepath.Join(dir, "app.log")
224			}
225		}
226		return ""
227	}
228	
229	func runMigrations(ctx context.Context, pool *pgxpool.Pool, logger *zap.Logger) error {
230		migrationDir := "/app/migrations"
231		if _, err := os.Stat(migrationDir); os.IsNotExist(err) {
232			migrationDir = "migrations"
233		}
234	
235		_, err := pool.Exec(ctx, `
236			CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS schema_migrations (
237				version INT PRIMARY KEY,
238				applied_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
239			)
240		`)
241		if err != nil {
242			return fmt.Errorf("create migration table: %w", err)
243		}
244	
245		entries, err := os.ReadDir(migrationDir)
246		if err != nil {
247			logger.Warn("no migration directory found", zap.String("dir", migrationDir))
248			return nil
249		}
250	
251		for _, entry := range entries {
252			if entry.IsDir() || !strings.HasSuffix(entry.Name(), ".up.sql") {
253				continue
254			}
255	
256			var version int
257			fmt.Sscanf(entry.Name(), "%d_", &version)
258			if version == 0 {
259				continue
260			}
261	
262			var applied bool
263			pool.QueryRow(ctx, `SELECT EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM schema_migrations WHERE version = $1)`, version).Scan(&applied)
264			if applied {
265				continue
266			}
267	
268			content, err := os.ReadFile(filepath.Join(migrationDir, entry.Name()))
269			if err != nil {
270				return fmt.Errorf("read migration %s: %w", entry.Name(), err)
271			}
272	
273			_, err = pool.Exec(ctx, string(content))
274			if err != nil {
275				return fmt.Errorf("execute migration %s: %w", entry.Name(), err)
276			}
277	
278			_, err = pool.Exec(ctx, `INSERT INTO schema_migrations (version) VALUES ($1)`, version)
279			if err != nil {
280				return fmt.Errorf("record migration %s: %w", entry.Name(), err)
281			}
282	
283			logger.Info("applied migration", zap.String("file", entry.Name()))
284		}
285	
286		return nil
287	}

79	    -H "Content-Type: application/json" -H "X-CSRF-Token: $SELLER_CSRF" \
80	    -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/collectibles" \
81	    -d '{"title":"Duplicate NFT","price_cents":5000,"contract_address":"0x1111111111111111111111111111111111111111","chain_id":137,"token_id":"unique-tok-001"}')
82	HTTP_CODE=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | tail -1)
83	assert_status "Duplicate collectible identity rejected (409)" "409" "$HTTP_CODE"
84	
85	# Without contract_address, chain_id and token_id auto-generated
86	RESPONSE=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" -b "$SELLER_JAR" \
87	    -H "Content-Type: application/json" -H "X-CSRF-Token: $SELLER_CSRF" \
88	    -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/collectibles" \
89	    -d '{"title":"Auto Identity","price_cents":2000}')
90	HTTP_CODE=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | tail -1)
91	BODY=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | sed '$d')
92	assert_status "Auto-identity collectible created (201)" "201" "$HTTP_CODE"
93	AUTO_CHAIN=$(echo "$BODY" | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin).get('chain_id',0))" 2>/dev/null)
94	AUTO_TOKEN=$(echo "$BODY" | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin).get('token_id',''))" 2>/dev/null)
95	if [ "$AUTO_CHAIN" = "1" ] && [ -n "$AUTO_TOKEN" ] && [ "$AUTO_TOKEN" != "" ]; then
96	    echo "  PASS: Auto-generated chain_id=1 and token_id ($AUTO_TOKEN)"
97	    PASS=$((PASS + 1))
98	else
99	    echo "  FAIL: Expected auto-generated chain_id=1 and non-empty token_id (got chain=$AUTO_CHAIN token=$AUTO_TOKEN)"
100	    FAIL=$((FAIL + 1))
101	fi
102	
103	# =============================================
104	# 2. Checkout Failure Events (overselling)
105	# =============================================
106	echo ""
107	echo "--- Checkout Failure Events ---"
108	
109	# Create a collectible and place first order
110	IDEM1=$(python3 -c "import uuid; print(uuid.uuid4())")
111	RESPONSE=$(curl -s -b "$SELLER_JAR" \
112	    -H "Content-Type: application/json" -H "X-CSRF-Token: $SELLER_CSRF" \
113	    -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/collectibles" \
114	    -d '{"title":"Oversell Test","price_cents":3000}')
115	OVERSELL_ID=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin).get('id',''))" 2>/dev/null)
116	
117	if [ -n "$OVERSELL_ID" ] && [ "$OVERSELL_ID" != "" ] && [ "$OVERSELL_ID" != "None" ]; then
118	    # First order succeeds
119	    RESPONSE=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" -b "$BUYER_JAR" \
120	        -H "Content-Type: application/json" -H "X-CSRF-Token: $BUYER_CSRF" \
121	        -H "Idempotency-Key: $IDEM1" \
122	        -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/orders" \
123	        -d "{\"collectible_id\":\"$OVERSELL_ID\"}")
124	    HTTP_CODE=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | tail -1)
125	    assert_status "First order succeeds (201)" "201" "$HTTP_CODE"
126	
127	    # Second order on same item should fail with oversold (409)
128	    IDEM2=$(python3 -c "import uuid; print(uuid.uuid4())")
129	    RESPONSE=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" -b "$BUYER_JAR" \
130	        -H "Content-Type: application/json" -H "X-CSRF-Token: $BUYER_CSRF" \
131	        -H "Idempotency-Key: $IDEM2" \
132	        -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/orders" \
133	        -d "{\"collectible_id\":\"$OVERSELL_ID\"}")
134	    HTTP_CODE=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | tail -1)
135	    BODY=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | sed '$d')
136	    assert_status "Second order rejected as oversold (409)" "409" "$HTTP_CODE"
137	    assert_json "Error code is ERR_OVERSOLD" "$BODY" "d['error']['code']" "ERR_OVERSOLD"
138	fi
139	
140	# =============================================
141	# 3. A/B Test → Assignment → Event Tagging
142	# =============================================
143	echo ""
144	echo "--- A/B Test End-to-End ---"
145	
146	# Create a running A/B test using a registered experiment
147	RESPONSE=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" -b "$ADMIN_JAR" \
148	    -H "Content-Type: application/json" -H "X-CSRF-Token: $ADMIN_CSRF" \
149	    -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/ab-tests" \
150	    -d '{
151	        "name":"catalog_layout","description":"End to end test",
152	        "traffic_pct":100,"start_date":"2020-01-01T00:00","end_date":"2099-12-31T23:59",
153	        "control_variant":"grid","test_variant":"list","rollback_threshold_pct":50
154	    }')
155	HTTP_CODE=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | tail -1)
156	BODY=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | sed '$d')
157	assert_status "Create running A/B test (201)" "201" "$HTTP_CODE"
158	AB_TEST_ID=$(echo "$BODY" | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin).get('id',''))" 2>/dev/null)
159	
160	# Get assignments as buyer — should include the test
161	RESPONSE=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" -b "$BUYER_JAR" \
162	    -H "X-CSRF-Token: $BUYER_CSRF" \
163	    "$BASE_URL/api/ab-tests/assignments")
164	HTTP_CODE=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | tail -1)
165	BODY=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | sed '$d')
166	assert_status "Get A/B assignments (200)" "200" "$HTTP_CODE"
167	
168	HAS_E2E=$(echo "$BODY" | python3 -c "
169	import sys,json
170	data=json.load(sys.stdin)
171	found=any(a['test_name']=='catalog_layout' for a in data)
172	print(found)
173	" 2>/dev/null)
174	if [ "$HAS_E2E" = "True" ]; then
175	    echo "  PASS: Buyer assigned to catalog_layout"
176	    PASS=$((PASS + 1))
177	else
178	    echo "  FAIL: Buyer should be assigned to catalog_layout"
179	    FAIL=$((FAIL + 1))
180	fi
181	
182	# Trigger a view event (which should be tagged with the A/B variant)
183	curl -s -b "$BUYER_JAR" -H "X-CSRF-Token: $BUYER_CSRF" \
184	    "$BASE_URL/api/collectibles/10000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001" > /dev/null
185	sleep 1
186	
187	# Rollback the test
188	if [ -n "$AB_TEST_ID" ] && [ "$AB_TEST_ID" != "None" ]; then
189	    RESPONSE=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" -b "$ADMIN_JAR" \
190	        -H "Content-Type: application/json" -H "X-CSRF-Token: $ADMIN_CSRF" \
191	        -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/ab-tests/$AB_TEST_ID/rollback")
192	    HTTP_CODE=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | tail -1)
193	    assert_status "Rollback A/B test (200)" "200" "$HTTP_CODE"
194	
195	    # Verify it's rolled back — can't rollback again
196	    RESPONSE=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" -b "$ADMIN_JAR" \
197	        -H "Content-Type: application/json" -H "X-CSRF-Token: $ADMIN_CSRF" \
198	        -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/ab-tests/$AB_TEST_ID/rollback")
199	    HTTP_CODE=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | tail -1)
200	    assert_status "Double rollback fails (422)" "422" "$HTTP_CODE"
201	fi
202	
203	# =============================================
204	# 4. Notification Lifecycle
205	# =============================================
206	echo ""
207	echo "--- Notification Lifecycle ---"
208	
209	# Trigger a notification by confirming an order
210	NOTIF_IDEM=$(python3 -c "import uuid; print(uuid.uuid4())")
211	RESPONSE=$(curl -s -b "$SELLER_JAR" \
212	    -H "Content-Type: application/json" -H "X-CSRF-Token: $SELLER_CSRF" \
213	    -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/collectibles" \
214	    -d '{"title":"Notif Test Item","price_cents":1000}')
215	NOTIF_CID=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin).get('id',''))" 2>/dev/null)
216	
217	if [ -n "$NOTIF_CID" ] && [ "$NOTIF_CID" != "None" ]; then
218	    # Buyer places order
219	    RESPONSE=$(curl -s -b "$BUYER_JAR" \
220	        -H "Content-Type: application/json" -H "X-CSRF-Token: $BUYER_CSRF" \
221	        -H "Idempotency-Key: $NOTIF_IDEM" \
222	        -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/orders" \
223	        -d "{\"collectible_id\":\"$NOTIF_CID\"}")
224	    NOTIF_OID=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin).get('id',''))" 2>/dev/null)
225	
226	    if [ -n "$NOTIF_OID" ] && [ "$NOTIF_OID" != "None" ]; then
227	        # Seller confirms — triggers notification to buyer
228	        curl -s -b "$SELLER_JAR" \
229	            -H "Content-Type: application/json" -H "X-CSRF-Token: $SELLER_CSRF" \
230	            -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/orders/$NOTIF_OID/confirm" > /dev/null
231	
232	        sleep 1
233	
234	        # Check buyer has notifications
235	        RESPONSE=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" -b "$BUYER_JAR" \
236	            -H "X-CSRF-Token: $BUYER_CSRF" \
237	            "$BASE_URL/api/notifications")
238	        HTTP_CODE=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | tail -1)
239	        BODY=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | sed '$d')
240	        assert_status "Buyer has notifications (200)" "200" "$HTTP_CODE"
241	
242	        NOTIF_COUNT=$(echo "$BODY" | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin).get('total_count',0))" 2>/dev/null)
243	        if [ "$NOTIF_COUNT" -gt 0 ] 2>/dev/null; then
244	            echo "  PASS: Buyer has $NOTIF_COUNT notification(s)"
245	            PASS=$((PASS + 1))
246	        else
247	            echo "  FAIL: Buyer should have notifications after order confirmed"
248	            FAIL=$((FAIL + 1))
249	        fi
250	    fi
251	fi
252	
253	# =============================================
254	# 5. Dashboard Owned Items = Completed Purchases
255	# =============================================
256	echo ""
257	echo "--- Dashboard Owned Items ---"
258	
259	RESPONSE=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" -b "$BUYER_JAR" \
260	    -H "X-CSRF-Token: $BUYER_CSRF" \
261	    "$BASE_URL/api/dashboard")
262	HTTP_CODE=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | tail -1)
263	BODY=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | sed '$d')
264	assert_status "Dashboard returns 200" "200" "$HTTP_CODE"
265	
266	# owned_collectibles should reflect completed orders (not seller listings)
267	HAS_FIELD=$(echo "$BODY" | python3 -c "import sys,json; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print('owned_collectibles' in d)" 2>/dev/null)
268	if [ "$HAS_FIELD" = "True" ]; then
269	    echo "  PASS: Dashboard includes owned_collectibles field"
270	    PASS=$((PASS + 1))
271	else
272	    echo "  FAIL: Dashboard should include owned_collectibles"
273	    FAIL=$((FAIL + 1))
274	fi
275	
276	# =============================================
277	# 6. Encrypted Config Support
278	# =============================================
279	echo ""
280	echo "--- Config Security ---"
281	# We can't test the actual encrypted keyfile loading at runtime since the
282	# server is already running with env vars. But we verify the code accepts
283	# development mode by checking the /api/auth/login endpoint still works
284	# (proving the config loaded successfully with plaintext env in dev mode).
285	RESPONSE=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/auth/login" \
286	    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
287	    -d '{"username":"admin","password":"testpass123"}')
288	HTTP_CODE=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | tail -1)
289	assert_status "Server running with config (login works)" "200" "$HTTP_CODE"
290	
291	echo ""
292	echo "=== E2E Hardening Tests Summary: $PASS passed, $FAIL failed ==="
293	exit $FAIL
294	
[result-id: r4]

1	#!/bin/bash
2	# API tests for rolling-window login lockout policy.
3	# Covers: threshold enforcement, lockout release via admin unlock,
4	#         window expiry (via direct DB manipulation), and post-unlock login.
5	# Requires: services running, seed data loaded, admin account available.
6	
7	BASE_URL="${API_BASE_URL:-http://localhost:8080}"
8	DB_URL="${DATABASE_URL:-postgresql://ledgermint:ledgermint@localhost:5432/ledgermint?sslmode=disable}"
9	PASS=0
10	FAIL=0
11	
12	assert_status() {
13	    local test_name="$1" expected="$2" actual="$3"
14	    if [ "$expected" = "$actual" ]; then
15	        echo "  PASS: $test_name (HTTP $actual)"
16	        PASS=$((PASS + 1))
17	    else
18	        echo "  FAIL: $test_name (expected HTTP $expected, got HTTP $actual)"
19	        FAIL=$((FAIL + 1))
20	    fi
21	}
22	
23	assert_json_field() {
24	    local test_name="$1" body="$2" field="$3" expected="$4"
25	    local actual
26	    actual=$(echo "$body" | python3 -c "import sys,json; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(d${field})" 2>/dev/null)
27	    if [ "$actual" = "$expected" ]; then
28	        echo "  PASS: $test_name"
29	        PASS=$((PASS + 1))
30	    else
31	        echo "  FAIL: $test_name (expected '$expected', got '$actual')"
32	        FAIL=$((FAIL + 1))
33	    fi
34	}
35	
36	echo "=== Rolling-Window Login Lockout Tests ==="
37	
38	# Log in as admin
39	ADMIN_JAR=$(mktemp)
40	curl -s -c "$ADMIN_JAR" \
41	    -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/auth/login" \
42	    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
43	    -d '{"username":"admin","password":"testpass123"}' > /dev/null
44	ADMIN_CSRF=$(grep csrf_token "$ADMIN_JAR" | awk '{print $NF}')
45	
46	cleanup() { rm -f "$ADMIN_JAR"; }
47	trap cleanup EXIT
48	
49	# ------------------------------------------------------------------
50	# Test group 1: Five failures in window → lockout on 6th attempt
51	# ------------------------------------------------------------------
52	echo ""
53	echo "--- Threshold Enforcement ---"
54	
55	LOCKOUT_USER="lockwin_$(date +%s)"
56	RESPONSE=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" -b "$ADMIN_JAR" \
57	    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
58	    -H "X-CSRF-Token: $ADMIN_CSRF" \
59	    -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/users" \
60	    -d "{\"username\":\"$LOCKOUT_USER\",\"password\":\"testpass123\",\"display_name\":\"Lockout Window Test\"}")
61	HTTP_CODE=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | tail -1)
62	BODY=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | sed '$d')
63	LOCKOUT_USER_ID=$(echo "$BODY" | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin).get('id',''))" 2>/dev/null)
64	
65	if [ "$HTTP_CODE" != "201" ] || [ -z "$LOCKOUT_USER_ID" ] || [ "$LOCKOUT_USER_ID" = "" ]; then
66	    echo "  SKIP: Could not create test user (HTTP $HTTP_CODE)"
67	    echo "=== Rolling-Window Lockout Tests Summary: 0 passed, 0 failed (skipped) ==="
68	    exit 0
69	fi
70	
71	echo "  Created test user: $LOCKOUT_USER ($LOCKOUT_USER_ID)"
72	
73	# Send 5 failed logins (should all return 401)
74	for i in 1 2 3 4 5; do
75	    RESPONSE=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" \
76	        -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/auth/login" \
77	        -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
78	        -d "{\"username\":\"$LOCKOUT_USER\",\"password\":\"wrongpassword\"}")
79	    HTTP_CODE=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | tail -1)
80	    assert_status "Failed login attempt $i returns 401" "401" "$HTTP_CODE"
81	done
82	
83	# 6th attempt — should be locked
84	RESPONSE=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" \
85	    -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/auth/login" \
86	    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
87	    -d "{\"username\":\"$LOCKOUT_USER\",\"password\":\"wrongpassword\"}")
88	HTTP_CODE=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | tail -1)
89	BODY=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | sed '$d')
90	assert_status "6th attempt returns 423 (locked)" "423" "$HTTP_CODE"
91	assert_json_field "Locked response error code" "$BODY" "['error']['code']" "ERR_ACCOUNT_LOCKED"
92	
93	# Correct password also rejected while locked
94	RESPONSE=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" \
95	    -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/auth/login" \
96	    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
97	    -d "{\"username\":\"$LOCKOUT_USER\",\"password\":\"testpass123\"}")
98	HTTP_CODE=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | tail -1)
99	assert_status "Correct password rejected while locked (423)" "423" "$HTTP_CODE"
100	
101	# ------------------------------------------------------------------
102	# Test group 2: Admin unlock → login succeeds
103	# ------------------------------------------------------------------
104	echo ""
105	echo "--- Lockout Release via Admin Unlock ---"
106	
107	RESPONSE=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" -b "$ADMIN_JAR" \
108	    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
109	    -H "X-CSRF-Token: $ADMIN_CSRF" \
110	    -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/users/$LOCKOUT_USER_ID/unlock")
111	HTTP_CODE=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | tail -1)
112	assert_status "Admin unlock succeeds (200)" "200" "$HTTP_CODE"
113	
114	RESPONSE=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" \
115	    -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/auth/login" \
116	    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
117	    -d "{\"username\":\"$LOCKOUT_USER\",\"password\":\"testpass123\"}")
118	HTTP_CODE=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | tail -1)
119	assert_status "Login succeeds after admin unlock (200)" "200" "$HTTP_CODE"
120	
121	# ------------------------------------------------------------------
122	# Test group 3: Window expiry — old attempts don't count
123	# Moves existing login_attempts rows back 20 minutes (outside the
124	# 15-minute window), then verifies login is allowed.
125	# ------------------------------------------------------------------
126	echo ""
127	echo "--- Rolling Window Expiry ---"
128	
129	EXPIRY_USER="lockexp_$(date +%s)"
130	RESPONSE=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" -b "$ADMIN_JAR" \
131	    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
132	    -H "X-CSRF-Token: $ADMIN_CSRF" \
133	    -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/users" \
134	    -d "{\"username\":\"$EXPIRY_USER\",\"password\":\"testpass123\",\"display_name\":\"Expiry Test\"}")
135	HTTP_CODE=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | tail -1)
136	BODY=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | sed '$d')
137	EXPIRY_USER_ID=$(echo "$BODY" | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin).get('id',''))" 2>/dev/null)
138	
139	if [ "$HTTP_CODE" != "201" ] || [ -z "$EXPIRY_USER_ID" ] || [ "$EXPIRY_USER_ID" = "" ]; then
140	    echo "  SKIP: Could not create expiry test user (HTTP $HTTP_CODE)"
141	else
142	    echo "  Created expiry test user: $EXPIRY_USER ($EXPIRY_USER_ID)"
143	
144	    # Send 4 failed logins (just under threshold)
145	    for i in 1 2 3 4; do
146	        curl -s -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/auth/login" \
147	            -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
148	            -d "{\"username\":\"$EXPIRY_USER\",\"password\":\"wrongpassword\"}" > /dev/null
149	    done
150	
151	    # Move those 4 attempts back 20 minutes (outside the 15-min window)
152	    psql "$DB_URL" -q -c \
153	        "UPDATE login_attempts SET attempted_at = attempted_at - INTERVAL '20 minutes' WHERE user_id = '$EXPIRY_USER_ID';" 2>/dev/null
154	
155	    if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then
156	        echo "  SKIP: Cannot manipulate DB directly (psql not available)"
157	    else
158	        # Now send 4 more failures — only these are inside the window,
159	        # so we should NOT be locked (threshold is 5).
160	        for i in 1 2 3 4; do
161	            curl -s -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/auth/login" \
162	                -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
163	                -d "{\"username\":\"$EXPIRY_USER\",\"password\":\"wrongpassword\"}" > /dev/null
164	        done
165	
166	        # Login with correct password should still succeed (only 4 in-window failures)
167	        RESPONSE=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" \
168	            -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/auth/login" \
169	            -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
170	            -d "{\"username\":\"$EXPIRY_USER\",\"password\":\"testpass123\"}")
171	        HTTP_CODE=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | tail -1)
172	        assert_status "Login succeeds when old attempts outside window (200)" "200" "$HTTP_CODE"
173	    fi
174	fi
175	
176	# ------------------------------------------------------------------
177	# Test group 4: Successful login clears the window
178	# After a successful login the failure count resets, so the user
179	# can sustain another round of failures before locking.
180	# ------------------------------------------------------------------
181	echo ""
182	echo "--- Successful Login Clears Window ---"
183	
184	CLEAR_USER="lockclear_$(date +%s)"
185	RESPONSE=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" -b "$ADMIN_JAR" \
186	    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
187	    -H "X-CSRF-Token: $ADMIN_CSRF" \
188	    -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/users" \
189	    -d "{\"username\":\"$CLEAR_USER\",\"password\":\"testpass123\",\"display_name\":\"Clear Test\"}")
190	HTTP_CODE=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | tail -1)
191	BODY=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | sed '$d')
192	CLEAR_USER_ID=$(echo "$BODY" | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin).get('id',''))" 2>/dev/null)
193	
194	if [ "$HTTP_CODE" != "201" ] || [ -z "$CLEAR_USER_ID" ] || [ "$CLEAR_USER_ID" = "" ]; then
195	    echo "  SKIP: Could not create clear test user (HTTP $HTTP_CODE)"
196	else
197	    echo "  Created clear test user: $CLEAR_USER ($CLEAR_USER_ID)"
198	
199	    # 4 failed attempts (just under threshold)
200	    for i in 1 2 3 4; do
201	        curl -s -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/auth/login" \
202	            -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
203	            -d "{\"username\":\"$CLEAR_USER\",\"password\":\"wrongpassword\"}" > /dev/null
204	    done
205	
206	    # Successful login should clear the window
207	    RESPONSE=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" \
208	        -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/auth/login" \
209	        -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
210	        -d "{\"username\":\"$CLEAR_USER\",\"password\":\"testpass123\"}")
211	    HTTP_CODE=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | tail -1)
212	    assert_status "Login succeeds with 4 failures (under threshold)" "200" "$HTTP_CODE"
213	
214	    # Now another 4 failures — should NOT trigger lock because window was cleared
215	    for i in 1 2 3 4; do
216	        curl -s -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/auth/login" \
217	            -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
218	            -d "{\"username\":\"$CLEAR_USER\",\"password\":\"wrongpassword\"}" > /dev/null
219	    done
220	
221	    RESPONSE=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" \
222	        -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/auth/login" \
223	        -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
224	        -d "{\"username\":\"$CLEAR_USER\",\"password\":\"testpass123\"}")
225	    HTTP_CODE=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | tail -1)
226	    assert_status "Login succeeds after window cleared by prior success (200)" "200" "$HTTP_CODE"
227	fi
228	
229	echo ""
230	echo "=== Rolling-Window Lockout Tests Summary: $PASS passed, $FAIL failed ==="
231	exit $FAIL
232

1	#!/bin/bash
2	# API tests for collectible endpoints
3	# Requires: services running, seed data loaded
4	
5	BASE_URL="${API_BASE_URL:-http://localhost:8080}"
6	PASS=0
7	FAIL=0
8	
9	assert_status() {
10	    local test_name="$1" expected="$2" actual="$3"
11	    if [ "$expected" = "$actual" ]; then
12	        echo "  PASS: $test_name (HTTP $actual)"
13	        PASS=$((PASS + 1))
14	    else
15	        echo "  FAIL: $test_name (expected HTTP $expected, got HTTP $actual)"
16	        FAIL=$((FAIL + 1))
17	    fi

20	assert_json_field() {
21	    local test_name="$1" body="$2" field="$3" expected="$4"
22	    local actual
23	    actual=$(echo "$body" | python3 -c "import sys,json; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(d${field})" 2>/dev/null)
24	    if [ "$actual" = "$expected" ]; then
25	        echo "  PASS: $test_name"

27	    else
28	        echo "  FAIL: $test_name (expected '$expected', got '$actual')"
29	        FAIL=$((FAIL + 1))
30	    fi
31	}

33	assert_json_gt() {

37	    if [ "$actual" -gt "$min_val" ] 2>/dev/null; then
38	        echo "  PASS: $test_name ($actual > $min_val)"
39	        PASS=$((PASS + 1))
40	    else
41	        echo "  FAIL: $test_name (expected > $min_val, got '$actual')"
42	        FAIL=$((FAIL + 1))
43	    fi

1	#!/bin/bash
2	# Integration tests for security hardening:
3	# - Cross-user order access denied (object-level authorization)
4	# - Server-side PII detection blocks messages
5	# - Hidden collectibles not visible to non-admin
6	# - CSRF enforcement
7	# - A/B test datetime-local format accepted
8	
9	BASE_URL="${API_BASE_URL:-http://localhost:8080}"
10	PASS=0
11	FAIL=0
12	
13	assert_status() {
14	    local test_name="$1" expected="$2" actual="$3"
15	    if [ "$expected" = "$actual" ]; then
16	        echo "  PASS: $test_name (HTTP $actual)"
17	        PASS=$((PASS + 1))
18	    else
19	        echo "  FAIL: $test_name (expected HTTP $expected, got HTTP $actual)"
20	        FAIL=$((FAIL + 1))
21	    fi
22	}
23	
24	assert_json_field() {
25	    local test_name="$1" body="$2" field="$3" expected="$4"
26	    local actual
27	    actual=$(echo "$body" | python3 -c "import sys,json; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(d${field})" 2>/dev/null)
28	    if [ "$actual" = "$expected" ]; then
29	        echo "  PASS: $test_name"
30	        PASS=$((PASS + 1))
31	    else
32	        echo "  FAIL: $test_name (expected '$expected', got '$actual')"
33	        FAIL=$((FAIL + 1))
34	    fi
35	}
36	
37	# Login helpers
38	login_as() {
39	    local user="$1" jar="$2"
40	    curl -s -c "$jar" -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/auth/login" \
41	        -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
42	        -d "{\"username\":\"$user\",\"password\":\"testpass123\"}" > /dev/null
43	}
44	
45	get_csrf() {
46	    grep csrf_token "$1" | awk '{print $NF}'
47	}
48	
49	ADMIN_JAR=$(mktemp); SELLER_JAR=$(mktemp); BUYER_JAR=$(mktemp); ANALYST_JAR=$(mktemp)
50	login_as "admin" "$ADMIN_JAR"
51	login_as "seller1" "$SELLER_JAR"
52	login_as "buyer1" "$BUYER_JAR"
53	login_as "analyst1" "$ANALYST_JAR"
54	ADMIN_CSRF=$(get_csrf "$ADMIN_JAR")
55	SELLER_CSRF=$(get_csrf "$SELLER_JAR")
56	BUYER_CSRF=$(get_csrf "$BUYER_JAR")
57	ANALYST_CSRF=$(get_csrf "$ANALYST_JAR")
58	
59	cleanup() { rm -f "$ADMIN_JAR" "$SELLER_JAR" "$BUYER_JAR" "$ANALYST_JAR"; }
60	trap cleanup EXIT
61	
62	echo "=== Security Integration Tests ==="
63	
64	# =============================================
65	# 1. Object-Level Authorization on Orders
66	# =============================================
67	echo ""
68	echo "--- Object-Level Authorization ---"
69	
70	# Create an order as buyer
71	IDEM=$(python3 -c "import uuid; print(uuid.uuid4())")
72	RESPONSE=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" -b "$BUYER_JAR" \
73	    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
74	    -H "X-CSRF-Token: $BUYER_CSRF" \
75	    -H "Idempotency-Key: $IDEM" \
76	    -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/orders" \
77	    -d '{"collectible_id":"10000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001"}')
78	HTTP_CODE=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | tail -1)
79	BODY=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | sed '$d')
80	ORDER_ID=$(echo "$BODY" | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin).get('id',''))" 2>/dev/null)
81	
82	if [ -n "$ORDER_ID" ] && [ "$ORDER_ID" != "" ] && [ "$ORDER_ID" != "None" ]; then
83	    # Analyst (not buyer or seller) tries to view the order -> 403
84	    RESPONSE=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" -b "$ANALYST_JAR" \
85	        -H "X-CSRF-Token: $ANALYST_CSRF" \
86	        "$BASE_URL/api/orders/$ORDER_ID")
87	    HTTP_CODE=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | tail -1)
88	    assert_status "Non-participant cannot view order (403)" "403" "$HTTP_CODE"
89	
90	    # Analyst tries to cancel the order -> 403
91	    RESPONSE=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" -b "$ANALYST_JAR" \
92	        -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
93	        -H "X-CSRF-Token: $ANALYST_CSRF" \
94	        -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/orders/$ORDER_ID/cancel" \
95	        -d '{"reason":"not my order"}')
96	    HTTP_CODE=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | tail -1)
97	    assert_status "Non-participant cannot cancel order (403)" "403" "$HTTP_CODE"
98	
99	    # Buyer (participant) CAN view the order
100	    RESPONSE=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" -b "$BUYER_JAR" \
101	        -H "X-CSRF-Token: $BUYER_CSRF" \
102	        "$BASE_URL/api/orders/$ORDER_ID")
103	    HTTP_CODE=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | tail -1)
104	    assert_status "Buyer (participant) can view own order (200)" "200" "$HTTP_CODE"
105	
106	    # Seller (participant) CAN view the order
107	    RESPONSE=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" -b "$SELLER_JAR" \
108	        -H "X-CSRF-Token: $SELLER_CSRF" \
109	        "$BASE_URL/api/orders/$ORDER_ID")
110	    HTTP_CODE=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | tail -1)
111	    assert_status "Seller (participant) can view order (200)" "200" "$HTTP_CODE"
112	
113	    # Clean up: cancel the order
114	    curl -s -b "$BUYER_JAR" \
115	        -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
116	        -H "X-CSRF-Token: $BUYER_CSRF" \
117	        -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/orders/$ORDER_ID/cancel" \
118	        -d '{"reason":"test cleanup"}' > /dev/null
119	else
120	    echo "  SKIP: Could not create order for OLAC tests"
121	fi
122	
123	# =============================================
124	# 2. Server-Side PII Detection
125	# =============================================
126	echo ""
127	echo "--- Server-Side PII Detection ---"
128	
129	# Create order for message tests
130	IDEM2=$(python3 -c "import uuid; print(uuid.uuid4())")
131	RESPONSE=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" -b "$BUYER_JAR" \

137	BODY=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | sed '$d')

139

154	    HTTP_CODE=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | tail -1)
155	    assert_status "Message with phone number blocked at API (422)" "422" "$HTTP_CODE"
156	
157	    # Email in message body -> blocked
158	    RESPONSE=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" -b "$BUYER_JAR" \

162	    HTTP_CODE=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | tail -1)

164

168	        -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/orders/$MSG_ORDER_ID/messages" \
169	        -F "body=When will my order ship?")
170	    HTTP_CODE=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | tail -1)
171	    assert_status "Clean message passes PII check (201)" "201" "$HTTP_CODE"
172	
173	    # Cleanup
174	    curl -s -b "$BUYER_JAR" \
175	        -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
176	        -H "X-CSRF-Token: $BUYER_CSRF" \
177	        -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/orders/$MSG_ORDER_ID/cancel" \
178	        -d '{"reason":"test cleanup"}' > /dev/null
179	else

181	fi
182	
183	# =============================================
184	# 3. Hidden Collectible Visibility
185	# =============================================
186	echo ""
187	echo "--- Hidden Collectible Visibility ---"
188	
189	# Create a collectible as seller, then hide it as admin
190	RESPONSE=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" -b "$SELLER_JAR" \
191	    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
192	    -H "X-CSRF-Token: $SELLER_CSRF" \
193	    -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/collectibles" \
194	    -d '{"title":"Hidden Test Item","description":"Should not be visible","price_cents":1000,"currency":"USD"}')
195	HTTP_CODE=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | tail -1)
196	BODY=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | sed '$d')
197	HIDDEN_ID=$(echo "$BODY" | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin).get('id',''))" 2>/dev/null)
198	
199	if [ -n "$HIDDEN_ID" ] && [ "$HIDDEN_ID" != "" ] && [ "$HIDDEN_ID" != "None" ]; then
200	    # Admin hides the collectible

202	        -H "Content-Type: application/json" \

204	        -X PATCH "$BASE_URL/api/collectibles/$HIDDEN_ID/hide" \

206	
207	    # Buyer cannot see hidden collectible by ID

209	        -H "X-CSRF-Token: $BUYER_CSRF" \
210	        "$BASE_URL/api/collectibles/$HIDDEN_ID")
211	    HTTP_CODE=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | tail -1)
212	    assert_status "Buyer cannot see hidden collectible (404)" "404" "$HTTP_CODE"
213

215	    RESPONSE=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" -b "$ADMIN_JAR" \
216	        -H "X-CSRF-Token: $ADMIN_CSRF" \
217	        "$BASE_URL/api/collectibles/$HIDDEN_ID")
218	    HTTP_CODE=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | tail -1)
219	    assert_status "Admin can see hidden collectible (200)" "200" "$HTTP_CODE"
220

227	    HIDDEN_COUNT=$(echo "$BODY" | python3 -c "

231	hidden = [i for i in items if i.get('status')=='hidden']
232	print(len(hidden))

234	    if [ "$HIDDEN_COUNT" = "0" ]; then

237	    else

239	        FAIL=$((FAIL + 1))

242	    # Re-publish for cleanup
243	    curl -s -b "$ADMIN_JAR" \
244	        -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
245	        -H "X-CSRF-Token: $ADMIN_CSRF" \
246	        -X PATCH "$BASE_URL/api/collectibles/$HIDDEN_ID/publish" > /dev/null
247	else

249	fi

253	# =============================================
254	echo ""
255	echo "--- Analytics Pipeline ---"
256	
257	# View a collectible (generates item_view event)

291	RESPONSE=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" -b "$ADMIN_JAR" \

318	# =============================================
319	echo ""
320	echo "--- Error Response Format ---"

2	# Pre-flight check: verifies API is reachable and seed data is present.

44	    PASS=$((PASS + 1))

136	    echo "  Fix the issues above and re-run."

24	}

26	func Setup(e *echo.Echo, h Handlers, signingKey []byte, userStore *store.UserStore, hotCache *cache.HotCache, logger *zap.Logger) {
27		// Global middleware — applied to every route in declaration order.
28		// CSRF runs first (globally) but exempts /api/auth/login and
29		// /api/setup/admin. The setup guard is applied per-group below so
30		// that the setup endpoints remain reachable before any admin exists.
31		e.Use(middleware.RequestID(logger))
32		e.Use(middleware.StructuredLogger(logger))
33		e.Use(middleware.IPFilter(userStore, hotCache))
34		e.Use(middleware.CSRF())
35	
36		api := e.Group("/api")
37	
38		// Initial setup (public, CSRF-exempt, self-disabling once an admin exists)
39		setup := api.Group("/setup")
40		setup.GET("/status", h.Setup.Status)
41		setup.POST("/admin", h.Setup.Bootstrap)
42	
43		// Block all remaining API access until initial setup is complete.
44		setupGuard := middleware.RequireSetup(userStore)
45	
46		// Auth (public, but gated behind setup)
47		loginRL := middleware.LoginRateLimiter()
48		auth := api.Group("/auth", setupGuard)
49		auth.POST("/login", h.Auth.Login, loginRL.Middleware())
50		auth.POST("/refresh", h.Auth.Refresh)
51	
52		// Protected routes
53		protected := api.Group("", setupGuard, middleware.JWTAuth(signingKey))
54	
55		// Auth (protected)
56		protected.GET("/auth/me", h.Auth.Me)
57		protected.POST("/auth/logout", h.Auth.Logout)
58	
59		// Dashboard
60		protected.GET("/dashboard", h.Analytics.GetDashboard)
61	
62		// Users (admin only)
63		adminRole := middleware.RequireRole("administrator")
64		users := protected.Group("/users", adminRole)
65		users.POST("", h.User.Create)
66		users.GET("", h.User.List)
67		users.GET("/:id", h.User.Get)
68		users.PATCH("/:id", h.User.Update)
69		users.POST("/:id/roles", h.User.AddRole)
70		users.DELETE("/:id/roles/:roleId", h.User.RemoveRole)
71		users.POST("/:id/unlock", h.User.Unlock)
72	
73		// Collectibles
74		listingRL := middleware.ListingRateLimiter()
75		listingIPRL := middleware.ListingIPRateLimiter()
76		collectibles := protected.Group("/collectibles")
77		collectibles.GET("", h.Collectible.List)
78		collectibles.GET("/mine", h.Collectible.ListMine, middleware.RequireRole("seller"))
79		collectibles.GET("/:id", h.Collectible.Get)
80		collectibles.POST("", h.Collectible.Create, middleware.RequireRole("seller"), listingRL.Middleware(), listingIPRL.Middleware())
81		collectibles.PATCH("/:id", h.Collectible.Update, middleware.RequireRole("seller"))
82		collectibles.POST("/:id/reviews", h.Collectible.PostReview)
83		collectibles.PATCH("/:id/hide", h.Collectible.Hide, adminRole)
84		collectibles.PATCH("/:id/publish", h.Collectible.Publish, adminRole)
85

88		orderIPRL := middleware.OrderIPRateLimiter()
89		orders := protected.Group("/orders")
90		orders.POST("", h.Order.Create, middleware.RequireRole("buyer"), orderRL.Middleware(), orderIPRL.Middleware())
91		orders.GET("", h.Order.List)
92		orders.GET("/:id", h.Order.Get)
93		orders.POST("/:id/confirm", h.Order.Confirm, middleware.RequireRole("seller"))
94		orders.POST("/:id/process", h.Order.Process, middleware.RequireRole("seller"))
95		orders.POST("/:id/complete", h.Order.Complete, middleware.RequireRole("seller"))
96		orders.POST("/:id/cancel", h.Order.Cancel)
97		orders.POST("/:id/refund", h.Order.ApproveRefund, middleware.RequireRole("seller"))
98		orders.POST("/:id/arbitration", h.Order.OpenArbitration)
99		orders.PATCH("/:id/fulfillment", h.Order.UpdateFulfillment, middleware.RequireRole("seller"))
100	
101		// Messages
102		messageRL := middleware.MessageRateLimiter()
103		messageIPRL := middleware.MessageIPRateLimiter()
104		orders.GET("/:orderId/messages", h.Message.List)
105		orders.POST("/:orderId/messages", h.Message.Send, messageRL.Middleware(), messageIPRL.Middleware())
106		protected.GET("/messages/:messageId/attachment", h.Message.DownloadAttachment)
107	
108		// Notifications
109		notifications := protected.Group("/notifications")
110		notifications.GET("", h.Notification.List)
111		notifications.PATCH("/:id/read", h.Notification.MarkRead)
112		notifications.POST("/read-all", h.Notification.MarkAllRead)

133		abtests.POST("/:id/complete", h.ABTest.Complete, abtestRunRole)
134		abtests.POST("/:id/rollback", h.ABTest.Rollback, abtestRunRole)

136		protected.GET("/ab-tests/registry", h.ABTest.GetRegistry)      // Available to all authenticated users
137	
138		// Admin (administrator only)

140		admin.GET("/ip-rules", h.Admin.ListIPRules)

143		admin.GET("/metrics", h.Analytics.GetMetrics)

145		// Admin anomalies (administrator + compliance_analyst)

147		adminAnomalies.GET("/anomalies", h.Admin.ListAnomalies)
148		adminAnomalies.PATCH("/anomalies/:id/acknowledge", h.Admin.AcknowledgeAnomaly)
149	}

27	func (h *AnalyticsHandler) GetFunnel(c echo.Context) error {
28		days, _ := strconv.Atoi(c.QueryParam("days"))
29		if days <= 0 {
30			days = 7

32	
33		funnel, err := h.analyticsService.GetFunnel(c.Request().Context(), days)
34		if err != nil {
35			return mapError(c, err)
36		}

38	}

44		}
45

48			return mapError(c, err)

25			`SELECT id, username, password_hash, display_name, email_encrypted, email_hash,
26			        is_locked, locked_until, failed_login_count, created_by, created_at, updated_at

29			&u.IsLocked, &u.LockedUntil, &u.FailedLoginCount, &u.CreatedBy, &u.CreatedAt, &u.UpdatedAt,
30		)

32			return nil, nil
33		}
34		return &u, err
35	}

52	func (s *UserStore) Create(ctx context.Context, u *model.User) error {
53		return s.pool.QueryRow(ctx,
54			`INSERT INTO users (username, password_hash, display_name, email_encrypted, email_hash, created_by)
55			 VALUES ($1, $2, $3, $4, $5, $6)
56			 RETURNING id, created_at, updated_at`,
57			u.Username, u.PasswordHash, u.DisplayName, u.EmailEncrypted, u.EmailHash, u.CreatedBy,
58		).Scan(&u.ID, &u.CreatedAt, &u.UpdatedAt)

62		_, err := s.pool.Exec(ctx,
63			`UPDATE users SET display_name = $2, password_hash = $3, email_encrypted = $4,
64			        email_hash = $5, updated_at = NOW()
65			 WHERE id = $1`,

71	func (s *UserStore) List(ctx context.Context, page, pageSize int) ([]model.User, int, error) {
72		var total int
73		err := s.pool.QueryRow(ctx, `SELECT COUNT(*) FROM users`).Scan(&total)
74		if err != nil {
75			return nil, 0, err
76		}
77	
78		offset := (page - 1) * pageSize
79		rows, err := s.pool.Query(ctx,
80			`SELECT id, username, display_name, is_locked, locked_until, created_at, updated_at
81			 FROM users ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT $1 OFFSET $2`, pageSize, offset)
82		if err != nil {
83			return nil, 0, err
84		}
85		defer rows.Close()
86	
87		var users []model.User
88		for rows.Next() {
89			var u model.User
90			if err := rows.Scan(&u.ID, &u.Username, &u.DisplayName, &u.IsLocked, &u.LockedUntil, &u.CreatedAt, &u.UpdatedAt); err != nil {
91				return nil, 0, err
92			}
93			users = append(users, u)
94		}
95		return users, total, nil
96	}

126		}
127	
128		offset := (page - 1) * pageSize
129		rows, err := s.pool.Query(ctx,
130			`SELECT id, idempotency_key, buyer_id, collectible_id, seller_id, status,
131			        price_snapshot_cents, cancellation_reason, cancelled_by, fulfillment_tracking,
132			        created_at, updated_at
133			 FROM orders WHERE `+field+` = $1
134			 ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT $2 OFFSET $3`, id, pageSize, offset)
135		if err != nil {
136			return nil, 0, err
137		}
138		defer rows.Close()
139	
140		var orders []model.Order
141		for rows.Next() {
142			var o model.Order
143			if err := rows.Scan(&o.ID, &o.IdempotencyKey, &o.BuyerID, &o.CollectibleID, &o.SellerID,
144				&o.Status, &o.PriceSnapshotCents, &o.CancellationReason, &o.CancelledBy,
145				&o.FulfillmentTracking, &o.CreatedAt, &o.UpdatedAt); err != nil {
146				return nil, 0, err
147			}
148			orders = append(orders, o)
149		}
150		return orders, total, nil

23	func (s *AnalyticsStore) RecordEvent(ctx context.Context, e *model.AnalyticsEvent) error {
24		_, err := s.pool.Exec(ctx,
25			`INSERT INTO analytics_events (user_id, event_type, collectible_id, session_id, ab_variant, metadata)
26			 VALUES ($1, $2, $3, $4, $5, $6)`,
27			e.UserID, e.EventType, e.CollectibleID, e.SessionID, e.ABVariant, e.Metadata)
28		return err
29	}
30	
31	func (s *AnalyticsStore) GetFunnel(ctx context.Context, days int) (*dto.FunnelResponse, error) {
32		since := time.Now().AddDate(0, 0, -days)
33		var views, orders int64
34		err := s.pool.QueryRow(ctx,
35			`SELECT
36				COALESCE(SUM(CASE WHEN event_type IN ('item_view', 'catalog_view') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END), 0),
37				COALESCE(SUM(CASE WHEN event_type = 'order_created' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END), 0)
38			 FROM analytics_events WHERE created_at >= $1`, since).Scan(&views, &orders)
39		if err != nil {
40			return nil, err
41		}
42	
43		rate := float64(0)
44		if views > 0 {
45			rate = float64(orders) / float64(views)
46		}
47		return &dto.FunnelResponse{Views: views, Orders: orders, Rate: rate, Days: days}, nil
48	}
49	
50	func (s *AnalyticsStore) GetRetention(ctx context.Context, days int) ([]dto.RetentionCohort, error) {
51		rows, err := s.pool.Query(ctx,
52			`WITH cohorts AS (
53				SELECT user_id, DATE(MIN(created_at)) AS cohort_date
54				FROM analytics_events
55				WHERE user_id IS NOT NULL AND created_at >= NOW() - make_interval(days => $1)
56				GROUP BY user_id
57			),
58			activity AS (
59				SELECT DISTINCT user_id, DATE(created_at) AS activity_date
60				FROM analytics_events
61				WHERE user_id IS NOT NULL AND created_at >= NOW() - make_interval(days => $1)
62			)
63			SELECT c.cohort_date::text, COUNT(DISTINCT c.user_id) AS cohort_size,
64			       COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN a.activity_date > c.cohort_date THEN a.user_id END) AS retained
65			FROM cohorts c
66			LEFT JOIN activity a ON c.user_id = a.user_id
67			GROUP BY c.cohort_date
68			ORDER BY c.cohort_date`, days)
69		if err != nil {
70			return nil, err
71		}
72		defer rows.Close()
73	
74		var cohorts []dto.RetentionCohort
75		for rows.Next() {
76			var c dto.RetentionCohort
77			if err := rows.Scan(&c.CohortDate, &c.CohortSize, &c.RetainedCount); err != nil {
78				return nil, err
79			}
80			if c.CohortSize > 0 {
81				c.RetentionRate = float64(c.RetainedCount) / float64(c.CohortSize)
82			}
83			cohorts = append(cohorts, c)
84		}
85		return cohorts, nil
86	}
87	
88	func (s *AnalyticsStore) GetContentPerformance(ctx context.Context, limit int) ([]dto.ContentPerformance, error) {
89		rows, err := s.pool.Query(ctx,
90			`SELECT c.id, c.title,
91				COALESCE(SUM(CASE WHEN ae.event_type IN ('item_view', 'catalog_view') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END), 0) AS views,
92				COALESCE(SUM(CASE WHEN ae.event_type = 'order_created' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END), 0) AS orders
93			 FROM collectibles c
94			 LEFT JOIN analytics_events ae ON ae.collectible_id = c.id
95			 GROUP BY c.id, c.title
96			 ORDER BY views DESC
97			 LIMIT $1`, limit)
98		if err != nil {
99			return nil, err
100		}

14	// RequireSetup returns middleware that blocks all API requests (except the
15	// setup endpoints themselves) until at least one administrator account exists.
16	// Once an admin is detected the check is cached so it does not hit the
17	// database on every request.
18	func RequireSetup(userStore *store.UserStore) echo.MiddlewareFunc {
19		var (
20			mu       sync.RWMutex
21			complete bool
22			checked  time.Time
23		)
24	
25		isComplete := func(ctx context.Context) bool {
26			mu.RLock()
27			if complete {
28				mu.RUnlock()
29				return true
30			}
31			// Re-check at most once every 2 seconds to avoid hammering the DB.
32			stale := time.Since(checked) > 2*time.Second
33			mu.RUnlock()
34	
35			if !stale {
36				return false
37			}
38	
39			exists, err := userStore.AdminExists(ctx)
40			if err != nil {
41				return false
42			}
43	
44			mu.Lock()
45			checked = time.Now()
46			if exists {
47				complete = true
48			}
49			mu.Unlock()
50			return exists
51		}
52	
53		return func(next echo.HandlerFunc) echo.HandlerFunc {
54			return func(c echo.Context) error {
55				if isComplete(c.Request().Context()) {
56					return next(c)
57				}
58				return c.JSON(http.StatusServiceUnavailable, dto.ErrorResponse{
59					Error: dto.ErrorDetail{
60						Code:      dto.CodeSetupRequired,
61						Message:   "Initial setup required. Create an administrator account via POST /api/setup/admin before using the API.",
62						RequestID: GetRequestID(c),
63					},
64				})
65			}
66		}
67	}

1	package store
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"encoding/json"
6	
7		"github.com/google/uuid"
8		"github.com/jackc/pgx/v5/pgxpool"
9		"github.com/ledgermint/platform/internal/model"
10	)
11	
12	type AuditStore struct {
13		pool *pgxpool.Pool
14	}
15	
16	func NewAuditStore(pool *pgxpool.Pool) *AuditStore {
17		return &AuditStore{pool: pool}
18	}
19	
20	func (s *AuditStore) Log(ctx context.Context, entry *model.AuditLog) error {
21		_, err := s.pool.Exec(ctx,
22			`INSERT INTO audit_logs (actor_id, action, resource_type, resource_id, details, ip_address)
23			 VALUES ($1, $2, $3, $4, $5, $6::inet)`,
24			entry.ActorID, entry.Action, entry.ResourceType, entry.ResourceID, entry.Details, nullableIP(entry.IPAddress))
25		return err
26	}
27	
28	// LogEvent is a convenience wrapper that builds an AuditLog from args.
29	func (s *AuditStore) LogEvent(ctx context.Context, actorID *uuid.UUID, action, resourceType string, resourceID *uuid.UUID, details map[string]interface{}, ip string) {
30		detailsJSON, _ := json.Marshal(details)
31		// Best-effort: audit writes must never block or fail callers.
32		_ = s.Log(ctx, &model.AuditLog{
33			ActorID:      actorID,
34			Action:       action,
35			ResourceType: resourceType,
36			ResourceID:   resourceID,
37			Details:      detailsJSON,
38			IPAddress:    ip,
39		})
40	}
41	
42	func nullableIP(ip string) *string {
43		if ip == "" {
44			return nil
45		}
46		return &ip
47	}

1	package middleware
2	
3	import (
4		"net/http"
5	
6		"github.com/labstack/echo/v4"
7		"github.com/ledgermint/platform/internal/dto"
8	)
9	
10	// GlobalErrorHandler converts all Echo errors into consistent JSON format.
11	// This ensures no raw framework errors (HTML or plain text) leak to clients.
12	func GlobalErrorHandler(err error, c echo.Context) {
13		if c.Response().Committed {
14			return
15		}
16	
17		code := http.StatusInternalServerError
18		errCode := dto.CodeInternal
19		message := "internal server error"
20	
21		if he, ok := err.(*echo.HTTPError); ok {
22			code = he.Code
23	
24			// If the error message is already our ErrorResponse format, use it directly
25			if resp, ok := he.Message.(dto.ErrorResponse); ok {
26				c.JSON(code, resp)
27				return
28			}
29	
30			// Map common HTTP status codes to our error codes
31			switch code {
32			case http.StatusBadRequest:
33				errCode = dto.CodeValidation
34				message = "bad request"
35			case http.StatusUnauthorized:
36				errCode = dto.CodeUnauthorized
37				message = "unauthorized"
38			case http.StatusForbidden:
39				errCode = dto.CodeForbidden
40				message = "forbidden"
41			case http.StatusNotFound:
42				errCode = dto.CodeNotFound
43				message = "not found"
44			case http.StatusMethodNotAllowed:
45				errCode = dto.CodeValidation
46				message = "method not allowed"
47			case http.StatusTooManyRequests:
48				errCode = dto.CodeRateLimited
49				message = "rate limited"
50			case http.StatusServiceUnavailable:
51				errCode = dto.CodeInternal
52				message = "service unavailable"
53			}
54	
55			// Use the echo error message if it's a string
56			if msg, ok := he.Message.(string); ok && msg != "" {
57				message = msg
58			}
59		}
60	
61		c.JSON(code, dto.ErrorResponse{
62			Error: dto.ErrorDetail{
63				Code:      errCode,
64				Message:   message,
65				RequestID: GetRequestID(c),
66			},
67		})
68	}
69

11. **Error Response Format** (lines 323-341)
    - HTTP 404 returns: `{"error": {"code": "...", "message": "..."}}`

---

### SUMMARY OF MISSING ENDPOINTS

| Test File | Endpoints with HTTP 000 | Priority |
|-----------|-------------------------|----------|
| test_notifications.sh | `/api/notifications*`, `/api/dashboard` | HIGH |
| test_orders.sh | `/api/orders`, `/api/orders/{id}/*` | HIGH |
| test_security.sh | `/api/analytics/*`, `/api/collectibles/{id}/hide|publish` | HIGH |
| test_auth.sh | `/api/auth/refresh` | MEDIUM |
| test_consistency.sh | `/api/orders/{id}/complete`, transaction history | MEDIUM |
| test_e2e_hardening.sh | Collectible identity fields, A/B test variants | MEDIUM |
| test_high_risk_integration.sh | All above + anomaly detection | MEDIUM |
| test_login_lockout.sh | Rolling window window expiry logic | MEDIUM |

All endpoints require **CSRF token protection** via `X-CSRF-Token` header for state-changing operations (POST, PATCH, PUT).
